title: quercus lanuginosa subsp. crispata
intro: Quercus lanuginosa subsp. crispata, also known as the Japanese scrub oak, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of Quercus lanuginosa subsp. crispata typically involve the use of its leaves or bark, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of the plant's health benefits and potential uses, but it has shown promise as a natural remedy for various ailments.
